attack_chain.description: Establish a trusted relationship with a remote service using a compromised account.
attack_chain.mitigation: General Best Practices: Apply defense-in-depth principles: network segmentation, least-privilege access, endpoint detection and response (EDR), security awareness training, regular patching, and monitoring.

attack_chain.description: Use Remote Services to connect to the compromised account.
attack_chain.mitigation: Multi-factor Authentication: Enforce MFA for remote services (SMB, RDP, WinRM). Restrict lateral movement paths using network segmentation and privileged access workstations.

attack_chain.description: Use Alternate Authentication Material to bypass MFA on the compromised account.
attack_chain.mitigation: General Best Practices: Apply defense-in-depth principles: network segmentation, least-privilege access, endpoint detection and response (EDR), security awareness training, regular patching, and monitoring.

attack_chain.description: Obtain valid accounts using the compromised account's credentials.
attack_chain.mitigation: Multi-factor Authentication: Enforce MFA for all remote access and privileged accounts. Monitor for anomalous login patterns, impossible travel, and credential stuffing.

attack_chain.description: Compromise additional accounts using the obtained valid accounts.
attack_chain.mitigation: General Best Practices: Apply defense-in-depth principles: network segmentation, least-privilege access, endpoint detection and response (EDR), security awareness training, regular patching, and monitoring.

attack_chain.description: Steal or forge authentication certificates to gain access to sensitive areas.
attack_chain.mitigation: General Best Practices: Apply defense-in-depth principles: network segmentation, least-privilege access, endpoint detection and response (EDR), security awareness training, regular patching, and monitoring.

attack_chain.description: Use the compromised accounts to steal financial information.
attack_chain.mitigation: General Best Practices: Apply defense-in-depth principles: network segmentation, least-privilege access, endpoint detection and response (EDR), security awareness training, regular patching, and monitoring.

attack_chain.description: Establish a trusted relationship with a remote service using a compromised account to maintain persistence.
attack_chain.mitigation: General Best Practices: Apply defense-in-depth principles: network segmentation, least-privilege access, endpoint detection and response (EDR), security awareness training, regular patching, and monitoring.

attack_chain.description: Use the compromised accounts to maintain access to sensitive areas.
attack_chain.mitigation: Multi-factor Authentication: Enforce MFA for all remote access and privileged accounts. Monitor for anomalous login patterns, impossible travel, and credential stuffing.

attack_chain.description: Use Alternate Authentication Material to maintain access to the compromised accounts.
attack_chain.mitigation: General Best Practices: Apply defense-in-depth principles: network segmentation, least-privilege access, endpoint detection and response (EDR), security awareness training, regular patching, and monitoring.

attack_chain.description: Use Remote Services to maintain command and control over the compromised accounts.
attack_chain.mitigation: Multi-factor Authentication: Enforce MFA for remote services (SMB, RDP, WinRM). Restrict lateral movement paths using network segmentation and privileged access workstations.

attack_chain.description: Continue to steal financial information using the compromised accounts.
attack_chain.mitigation: General Best Practices: Apply defense-in-depth principles: network segmentation, least-privilege access, endpoint detection and response (EDR), security awareness training, regular patching, and monitoring.

retrieval_results.description:
Adversaries may use Valid Accounts to log into a service that accepts remote connections, such as telnet, SSH, and VNC. The adversary may then perform actions as the logged-on user.
